understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to complete each level by moving the selectable pieces so that every goal tile marked with a '+' in the semantic grid is occupied by a selectable piece at the same time; when all goals are covered simultaneously, the level (and ultimately the run) reports WIN.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement actions (MOVE_LEFT/RIGHT/UP/DOWN) shift the currently selected piece on the grid in fixed increments (3 pixels per cell), with walls/boundaries preventing movement in some directions. Some adjacent objects are pushable (e.g., blocks), so attempting to move into them can push them along if space allows; otherwise movement fails. The 'movement.*_blocked' flags are only hints about adjacency and do not perfectly predict whether a move will succeed. When multiple selectables exist, CLICK can switch control to another selectable, but it only works if the target_position exactly matches that selectable's pixel position from the state; clicking empty space or a non-matching coordinate has no effect.
